title: NATHEALTH expands its leadership team, appoints Treasurer and Secretary for 2026 – 27
summary: NATHEALTH, an apex body representing the Indian healthcare industry, announced the expansion of its leadership team for FY 2026–27 following its Governing Council (GC) meeting. Shankha Banerjee, CEO, Dr. Lal […]
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
The expanded leadership team now comprises Dr Sangita Reddy as President, Himanshu Baid as Senior Vice President, Varun Khanna as Vice President, Shankha Banerjee as Treasurer, and Mandeep Singh Kumar as Secretary.

NATHEALTH, an apex body representing the Indian healthcare industry, announced the expansion of its leadership team for FY 2026–27 following its Governing Council (GC) meeting. Shankha Banerjee, CEO, Dr. Lal Path Labs Ltd., has been appointed as Treasurer, and Mandeep Singh Kumar, Managing Director & Vice President, Medtronic India, has been appointed as Secretary, joining the previously announced leadership team led by Dr Sangita Reddy, Joint Managing Director, Apollo Hospitals Group, as President.

Siddhartha Bhattacharya will continue as Secretary General of NATHEALTH, leading the Secretariat’s efforts to build strategic partnerships, strengthen regional stakeholder engagement, and collaborate with policymakers to drive the next phase of healthcare development in India.

President of NATHEALTH – Dr Sangita Reddy, Joint Managing Director, Apollo Hospitals Group, said, “We are delighted to welcome Mr. Shankha Banerjee and Mr. Mandeep Singh Kumar to the NATHEALTH Leadership Team. Both bring rich and diverse experience from across the healthcare ecosystem, diagnostics and pathology on one hand, and medical technology on the other, which will greatly strengthen our collective ability to drive meaningful change. As India advances towards the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047, having leaders who understand the full continuum of care will be critical as we work to build a system that prioritises prevention, patient safety, and long-term wellbeing. I look forward to working closely with this team to advance NATHEALTH’s mission.”

Treasurer, NATHEALTH – Shankha Banerjee, CEO, Dr. Lal Path Labs Ltd. said, “Delivering better health outcomes in India will depend on strengthening early intervention, continuity of care, and data-driven decision-making across the healthcare system. As the burden of non-communicable diseases continues to rise, there is a clear need to shift from reactive treatment to proactive and preventive care. Diagnostics will play a critical enabling role in this transition by supporting early detection and guiding clinical decisions. I look forward to contributing to NATHEALTH’s efforts to build a more integrated, efficient, and patient-centric healthcare ecosystem.”

Secretary, NATHEALTH – Mandeep Singh Kumar, Managing Director & Vice President, Medtronic India said, “India’s healthcare ecosystem is at a pivotal moment, shaped by growing demand, evolving patient expectations, and rapid innovation. Building a future‑ready system will require sustained investment in strong infrastructure, improved clinical outcomes, and scalable efficiency. Progress at this scale can only be achieved through deep collaboration across government, industry, and care providers. Medical technology has a critical role to play in advancing precision, expanding access, and enabling better outcomes for patients. Through platforms such as NATHEALTH, I look forward to working with partners across the ecosystem to accelerate innovation and create lasting impact for healthcare in India.”

With this strengthened leadership, NATHEALTH aims to play a pivotal role in shaping the next phase of India’s healthcare transformation. As the country progresses towards the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047, the focus will remain on enabling innovation, strengthening health systems, and fostering public–private collaboration. NATHEALTH will continue to champion initiatives that improve access, enhance quality, and build a future-ready, patient-centric healthcare ecosystem.
